title: Enforce no-privileged-containers with Kyverno
scenario: A security audit found 3 Deployments running containers with `securityContext.privileged: true`. You've been asked to install a Kyverno ClusterPolicy that prevents any new pods from running privileged containers. The starter policy uses `validationFailureAction: audit` (logs violations but doesn't block them). Switch it to enforce.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged
spec:
  validationFailureAction: audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: no-privileged
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Privileged containers are not allowed"
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - =(securityContext):
              =(privileged): "false"

hints.text: `audit` mode logs violations to the `PolicyReport` CRD but lets pods through. Change to `Enforce` to actually block them at admission time.
Only one field to change: `validationFailureAction: audit` → `Enforce`.
Kyverno accepts both `Enforce` (capital E) and `enforce`. The pattern `=(securityContext)` means "if the field exists, check it" — pods without securityContext are allowed through.

solution:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Enforce
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: no-privileged
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Privileged containers are not allowed"
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - =(securityContext):
              =(privileged): "false"
